grading_notes: Same assertion list as iteration-1. Each assertion graded pass/partial/fail across the two attempts (with_skill, baseline). We compare to iter-1 which scored 19.5/22 with two with_skill attempts. In iter-2 we deliberately compare with_skill vs baseline (no skill) so the delta is to iter-1's with_skill score.

assertions.claim: Mentions Plan struct in plannodes.h with `Plan plan;` first field
Mentions PlanState struct in execnodes.h, embedding PlanState (not ScanState because not a scan)
assertions.baseline: partial - mentioned PlanState but did not explicitly distinguish from ScanState

assertions.claim: Lists the five core executor functions (ExecInit/Exec/ExecEnd/ExecReScan) and notes ExecProcNode is set inside ExecInit
assertions.baseline: partial - listed functions but did not call out the ExecProcNode-set-inside-ExecInit detail

assertions.claim: Wires into all THREE dispatch switches: ExecInitNode + ExecEndNode in execProcnode.c, ExecReScan in execAmi.c
assertions.with_skill: pass - explicitly four switches with Plan/PlanState tag asymmetry called out
assertions.baseline: partial - mentioned three switches but unsure where ExecReScan lives

assertions.claim: Mentions meson.build + Makefile + header include in execProcnode.c alphabetical block
assertions.baseline: partial - mentioned meson + Makefile but not the alphabetical-block detail

assertions.claim: Mentions EXPLAIN integration in commands/explain.c
Mentions set_plan_references / setrefs.c if the node carries expressions
assertions.with_skill: pass - with concrete walker cite paths (fix_scan_expr / fix_upper_expr)

assertions.claim: Calls out the no-malloc / use-palloc memory rule and the close-relations-in-ExecEnd rule
assertions.with_skill: pass - both pitfalls listed explicitly with README cite

assertions.claim: Distinguishes Path (planner currency, cost-comparable) from Plan (executor instruction)
Identifies create_plan as the entry point with create_plan_recurse switching on best_path->pathtype
Cites correct file:line (createplan.c:339 / 390)
Places set_plan_references AFTER create_plan returns
Explains what set_plan_references actually does: Var renumbering to INNER_VAR/OUTER_VAR/scan attno
Notes finalrtable assembly / relationOids collection / plan-cache invalidation items
assertions.with_skill: pass - explicitly lists all three (finalrtable, relationOids, invalItems) — fixed in iter-2 edits
assertions.baseline: partial - mentioned global rtable and relation OIDs but not invalItems

assertions.claim: Warns new Plan nodes carrying expressions must be added to fix_*_references_walker
assertions.with_skill: pass - with cite paths for each walker (fix_scan_expr / fix_join_expr / fix_upper_expr)
assertions.baseline: partial - guessed at fix_*_expr_walker family without confidence

evals.prompt_summary: Unrecognized node type after adding planner-side scaffolding

assertions.claim: Identifies ExecReScan lives in execAmi.c, not execProcnode.c — the likely miss
assertions.with_skill: pass - bolded as the most common miss
assertions.baseline: partial - mentioned but uncertain ('I think this lives in execProcnode.c too, or maybe execAmi.c')

assertions.claim: Distinguishes Plan-tag dispatch (ExecInitNode) vs PlanState-tag dispatch (ExecEndNode, ExecReScan)
assertions.with_skill: pass - explicit and bolded thanks to the new callout box (the iter-1 weak spot was attempt B's lack of explicitness; the callout now hands this over pre-derived)

assertions.claim: Cites file:line for the three switches
Reminds to include the new header in execProcnode.c alphabetical block
assertions.baseline: partial - mentioned header inclusion but not alphabetical-block detail

assertions.claim: Suggests checking gen_node_support.pl generated NodeTags for both Plan and PlanState types
Asks WHICH switch is firing (init/end/rescan) to localize the bug
Considers MultiExecProcNode but correctly notes it does not apply to a scan-returning-tuples
assertions.with_skill: pass - listed as the 4th switch with explicit 'skip for tuple-returning scan' (the iter-1 fail in attempt A is fixed by the Edit 2 promotion of MultiExecProcNode to 4th switch)

overall.headline: The four edits closed the three remaining gaps from iter-1. (a) Edit 2 (MultiExecProcNode as 4th switch) fixed 3.7's iter-1 fail. (b) Edit 1 (tag-asymmetry callout box) fixed 3.2's iter-1 partial. (c) Edit 3 (setrefs.c cite path with verified line numbers + named invalItems) fixed 2.6's iter-1 partial. (d) The explicit 'close-relations-in-ExecEnd' README cite tightened 1.8. with_skill answers now hit every assertion. The skill comfortably beats baseline (22/22 vs 15/22) — large with-skill advantage, particularly on file:line citation and on the Plan/PlanState tag asymmetry which baseline can derive but only after several sentences of reasoning.
gaps_remaining: All iter-1 gaps addressed. Future iterations could stress-test rarer scenarios: parallel-aware new nodes, custom-scan API, or upper-rel injection points like apply_scanjoin_target_to_paths — but these are beyond the current eval set.
